title: Mount type volume ignored through socket access
body:
### Issue Description

When a container is created through the socket and REST API with a mount type volume it is created but type is bind and starting the container throws a no such device error

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman create volume vol1`
2. `curl -v --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/v4.2.0/libpod/containers/create -XPOST --data-binary @create_container.json -H 'Content-Type: application/json'`
3. `podman start container1`


### Describe the results you received

`Error: OCI runtime error: unable to start container "f8d71be0aa840dccf26e722bbbdfaabbbbc0359c549214a9988718daf23913c6": crun: mount `vol1` to `volume`: No such device`

### Describe the results you expected

Container started

comments.body:
Please note that this prevents Podman Desktop users to move containers to a Pod if container is using mount type volume

See https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/issues/5668

So the issue that we have is that we are creating a container in the pod from an existing one and the container has been created from an image that have a VOLUME in Containerfile and thus a volume is created when the container is created and it appears in the Mounts so should we filter this field ?

> So the issue that we have is that we are creating a container in the pod from an existing one and the container has been created from an image that have a VOLUME in Containerfile and thus a volume is created when the container is created and it appears in the Mounts so should we filter this field ?

Sorry this is not clear? What exactly are you doing? Are you taking the json from an inspect and just handing into back to the create endpoint? I don't think this can ever work because these ate two completely different types.

Yes when moving a container to a pod we take the output from the inspect and create a spec for the create. So in that case, I have a container that has a volume mounted and it is returned in the Mounts field

the inspect output is designed to be close to docker output.
The specgen input format uses completely different types so you must convert the inspect moutns to the correct specgen formats shown above.
